2605.09908.title: Voice Biomarkers for Depression and Anxiety
2605.09908.paper_summary: Current approaches to detecting depression and anxiety from speech primarily rely on machine learning techniques that utilize hand-engineered paralinguistic features and related acoustic descriptors derived from time- and frequency-domain representations of speech signals. Applying deep learning methods directly to raw speech signals has the potential to produce biomarker representations with substantially greater predictive power. However, these approaches typically require large volumes of carefully annotated data to learn robust and clinically meaningful representations of the underlying biomarkers. In this paper, we describe our efforts toward developing a deep learning model trained on a large-scale proprietary dataset comprising ~65,000 utterances collected from more than 23,000 subjects representative of relevant United States demographics. We present the techniques employed and analyze their impact on model performance. Our results demonstrate that the proposed models can extract content-agnostic biomarker information, which, when combined with lexical features extracted from audio, yields improved predictive performance in production settings. Our models are evaluated on ~5000 unique subjects and achieve performance of 71% in terms of sensitivity and specificity. To foster further research in mental health assessment from speech, we release the best-performing model described in this paper on HuggingFace.
2605.09908.paper_summary_zh: 目前從語音中檢測抑鬱和焦慮的方式主要依賴於機器學習技術，這些技術利用手工設計的副語言特徵和相關的聲學描述符，這些特徵和描述符源自語音信號的時間和頻率域表示。<lb/>直接將深度學習方法應用於原始語音信號有潛力產生具有顯著更高預測能力的生物標記表示。<lb/>然而，這些方法通常需要大量經過仔細標註的數據，以學習穩健且具有臨床意義的潛在生物標記表示。<lb/>在本文中，我們描述了我們在開發一個基於大規模專有數據集訓練的深度學習模型方面的努力，該數據集包含約65,000個語句，這些語句來自超過23,000名代表美國相關人口統計的受試者。<lb/>我們介紹了所使用的技術並分析其對模型性能的影響。<lb/>我們的結果顯示，所提出的模型可以提取與內容無關的生物標記信息，當這些信息與從音頻中提取的詞彙特徵結合時，能在生產環境中提高預測性能。<lb/>我們的模型在約5000名獨特受試者上進行評估，並在靈敏度和特異性方面達到71%的性能。<lb/>為了促進從語音中進行心理健康評估的進一步研究，我們在HuggingFace上發布了本文中描述的表現最佳的模型。
2605.09908.author: Oleksii Abramenko et.al.
2605.09908.authors: Oleksii Abramenko, Noah D. Stein, Colin Vaz
